methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Ukrspecsystems operates in Defense & Security as a growth-stage company. The valuation exercise drew from a sector universe of 21 peers: 2 publicly traded (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private entities including Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI.

The peer-median method proved inapplicable. A search for same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables yielded insufficient datapoints—zero same-stage matches, one adjacent-stage match, and zero similar-tier peers. This shortfall fell below the three-datapoint threshold required for reliable triangulation. Application of an all-sector median would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneity of Defense & Security valuations across stage and scale.

Without sufficient narrow-set multiples or transaction precedent data provided, conventional triangulation cannot support a specific valuation range. The available comps establish directional reference points—the listed public and private peers anchor the sector landscape—but lack the density and stage-specificity necessary to derive defensible forward multiples or DCF-calibration benchmarks. Key uncertainties include the trajectory of Ukrspecsystems' MoD contract revenue, contract renewal risk, and differentiation relative to the identified peer set. Further financial detail (revenue, EBITDA, contract pipeline) and closer stage-matched comparables would be required to narrow a valuation range.
